title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruption, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Implement strict security controls and monitoring for CI/CD pipelines, including regular vulnerability assessments and access restrictions.
Ensure robust backup strategies are in place, with backups stored in isolated environments to prevent ransomware encryption.
Deploy advanced threat detection solutions to monitor for anomalous activities in cloud environments and respond to potential breaches promptly.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk. However, if improperly configured or exploited, it could expose sensitive dependency data or be leveraged by attackers to identify unpatched vulnerabilities in target systems.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and security practices before integrating it into your environment.
Use the tool in a controlled and isolated environment to prevent accidental exposure of sensitive data.
Ensure proper access controls and monitoring are in place when using the tool to scan dependencies.

title: Incorrect permission assignment for a critical resource in Armoury Crate allows a local user to...
description:
Incorrect permission assignment for a critical resource in Armoury Crate allows a local user to bypass the driver’s validation mechanism, resulting in unauthorized read and write access to physical memory.Refer to the ' 
Security Update for Armoury Crate App   ' section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8070.

title: An Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource vulnerability in ASUS System Control...
description:
An Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource vulnerability in ASUS System Control Interface allows a local user to elevate privileges to SYSTEM and execute arbitrary code via a crafted RPC call that bypass the validation mechanism.
Refer to the 'Security Update for ASUS System Control Interface' section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7480.

title: Improper handling of symbolic links in the installer of CUPS Printer Driver for macOS(*) may...
description:
Improper handling of symbolic links in the installer of CUPS Printer Driver for macOS(*) may allow a local attacker with login privileges to exploit a specially crafted symbolic link during installation to modify permissions of directories for which they would not normally have authorization. 

*:Canon PIXUS iX6800 Series CUPS Printer Driver for macOS Version 16.91.0.0 or earlier (Japan)

Canon PIXMA MG2500 Series and iX6800 Series CUPS Printer Driver for macOS Version 16.91.0.0 or earlier (US a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6892.

title: Improper handling of symbolic links in the installer of My Image Garden for macOS Version 3.6.8...
description: Improper handling of symbolic links in the installer of My Image Garden for macOS Version 3.6.8 or earlier may allow a local attacker with login privileges to exploit a specially crafted symbolic link during installation to modify permissions of files for which they would not normally have authorization.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a local attacker to exploit symbolic link handling during installation, potentially modifying file permissions and gaining unauthorized access to sensitive files. This could lead to privilege escalation or data integrity issues.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of My Image Garden as soon as a patched release is available.
Restrict local user access on macOS systems to trusted individuals only.
Monitor file permissions and audit system logs for any unauthorized changes during or after installation.

title: Use after free in Core in Google Chrome on Windows prior to 148.0.7778.216 allowed a remote...
description: Use after free in Core in Google Chrome on Windows prior to 148.0.7778.216 allowed a remote attacker who had compromised the renderer process to potentially perform a sandbox escape via a crafted HTML page. (Chromium security severity: High)

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a remote attacker to execute a sandbox escape by exploiting a use-after-free flaw in Google Chrome's Core component. This could lead to unauthorized access to the underlying system, data exfiltration, or further exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Google Chrome to version 148.0.7778.216 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Enable automatic updates for Google Chrome to ensure timely patching of future vulnerabilities.
Implement robust endpoint protection and monitoring to detect and respond to potential exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-39835 - SSH servers which use CertChecker as a public key callback without setting IsUserAuthority or IsHost
description: SSH servers which use CertChecker as a public key callback without setting IsUserAuthority or IsHostAuthority could be caused to panic by a client presenting a certificate. CertChecker now returns an error instead of panicking when these callbacks are nil.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39835.

title: CVE-2026-39834 - When writing data larger than 4GB in a single Write call on an SSH channel, an integer overflow in t
description: When writing data larger than 4GB in a single Write call on an SSH channel, an integer overflow in the internal payload size calculation caused the write loop to spin indefinitely, sending empty packets without making progress. The size comparison now uses int64 to prevent truncation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to exploit an integer overflow in SSH channel data handling, potentially causing a denial of service (DoS) by spinning the write loop indefinitely. This could disrupt critical SSH-based services and systems, leading to operational downtime and resource exhaustion.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patches or updates provided by the vendor to address CVE-2026-39834.
Monitor SSH server logs for unusual activity, such as repeated empty packet transmissions, and implement rate-limiting on SSH connections to mitigate potential abuse.
Conduct a security audit of systems using SSH to identify and mitigate any additional risks related to this vulnerability.

title: CVE-2026-39833 - The in-memory keyring returned by NewKeyring() silently accepted keys with the ConfirmBeforeUse cons
description: The in-memory keyring returned by NewKeyring() silently accepted keys with the ConfirmBeforeUse constraint but never enforced it. The key would sign without any confirmation prompt, with no indication to the caller that the constraint was not in effect. NewKeyring() now returns an error when unsupported constraints are requested.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39833.

title: CVE-2026-39832 - When adding a key to a remote agent constraint extensions such as restrict-destination-v00@openssh.c
description: When adding a key to a remote agent constraint extensions such as restrict-destination-v00@openssh.com were not serialized in the request. Destination restrictions were silently stripped when forwarding keys, allowing unrestricted use of the key on the remote host. The client now serializes all constraint extensions. Additionally, the in-memory keyring returned by NewKeyring() now rejects keys with unsupported constraint extensions instead of silently ignoring them.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39832.

title: CVE-2026-39831 - The Verify() method for FIDO/U2F security key types (sk-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256@openssh.com, sk-ssh-ed25
description: The Verify() method for FIDO/U2F security key types (sk-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256@openssh.com, sk-ssh-ed25519@openssh.com) did not check the User Presence flag. Signatures generated without physical touch were accepted, allowing unattended use of a hardware security key. To restore the previous behavior, return a "no-touch-required" extension in Permissions.Extensions from PublicKeyCallback.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39831.

title: CVE-2026-39830 - A malicious SSH peer could send unsolicited global request responses to fill an internal buffer, blo
description: A malicious SSH peer could send unsolicited global request responses to fill an internal buffer, blocking the connection's read loop. The blocked goroutine could not be released by calling Close(), resulting in a resource leak per connection. Unsolicited global responses are now discarded.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39830.

title: CVE-2026-39829 - The RSA and DSA public key parsers did not enforce size limits on key parameters. A crafted public k
description: The RSA and DSA public key parsers did not enforce size limits on key parameters. A crafted public key with an excessively large modulus or DSA parameter could cause several minutes of CPU consumption during signature verification. This could be triggered by unauthenticated clients during public key authentication. RSA moduli are now limited to 8192 bits, and DSA parameters are validated per FIPS 186-2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39829.

title: CVE-2026-39828 - When an SSH server authentication callback returned PartialSuccessError with non-nil Permissions, th
description: When an SSH server authentication callback returned PartialSuccessError with non-nil Permissions, those permissions were silently discarded, potentially dropping certificate restrictions such as force-command after a second factor succeeded. Returning non-nil Permissions with PartialSuccessError now results in a connection error.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39828.

title: CVE-2026-39827 - An authenticated SSH client that repeatedly opened channels which were rejected by the server caused
description: An authenticated SSH client that repeatedly opened channels which were rejected by the server caused unbounded memory growth, eventually crashing the server process and affecting all connected users. Rejected channels are now properly removed from the connection's internal state and released for garbage collection.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39827.

title: CVE-2026-9264 - A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in SketchUp 2026's Dynamic Components feature allows remo
description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in SketchUp 2026's Dynamic Components feature allows remote code execution and local file exfiltration through maliciously crafted SKP files. The vulnerability stems from improper input sanitization in the component options window, enabling attackers to execute arbitrary system commands and read local files without user interaction by exploiting an embedded Internet Explorer 11 browser.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9264.

title: CVE-2026-34911 - A malicious actor with access to the network and low privileges could exploit a Path Traversal vulne
description: A malicious actor with access to the network and low privileges could exploit a Path Traversal vulnerability found in UniFi OS devices to access files on the underlying system that could be manipulated to obtain sensitive information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-34911.

title: CVE-2026-34910 - A malicious actor with access to the network could exploit an Improper Input Validation vulnerabilit
description: A malicious actor with access to the network could exploit an Improper Input Validation vulnerability found in UniFi OS devices to execute a Command Injection.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-34910.

description: A malicious actor with access to the network could exploit a Path Traversal vulnerability found in UniFi OS devices to access files on the underlying system that could be manipulated to access an underlying account.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-34909.

description: A malicious actor with access to the network could exploit an Improper Access Control vulnerability found in UniFi OS devices to make unauthorized changes to the system.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-34908.

title: CVE-2026-33000 - A malicious actor with access to the network and high privileges could exploit an Improper Input Val
description: A malicious actor with access to the network and high privileges could exploit an Improper Input Validation vulnerability found in UniFi OS devices to execute a Command Injection.
